name: Mitosis vs meiosis — daughter cells
question_text: Starting from a single diploid somatic cell, mitosis produces how many daughter cells, and what is their ploidy?
general_feedback: Mitosis divides one diploid (2n) cell into two genetically identical diploid daughter cells. Meiosis, in contrast, produces four haploid (n) gametes from one diploid precursor.
correct_feedback: Correct — Mitosis yields two 2n cells, genetically identical to the parent.

answers.feedback: Mitosis yields two 2n cells, genetically identical to the parent.
That is the outcome of meiosis, not mitosis.
Mitosis produces two daughter cells, not four.
Mitosis does not halve chromosome number; the daughter cells remain diploid.
